name: valid_dm_unsatisfiable
code:
proof fn phi_1_valid_dm_unsatisfiable(dm: DelegationMap<AbstractKey>)
    requires
        dm.valid(),
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If valid() is unsatisfiable, all ensures of delegate_for_key_range_is_host_impl hold vacuously

name: delegation_always_true
code:
proof fn phi_2_delegation_always_true(dm: DelegationMap<AbstractKey>, lo: KeyIterator<AbstractKey>, hi: KeyIterator<AbstractKey>, dst: ID)
    requires
        dm.valid(),
    ensures
        AbstractDelegationMap::delegate_for_key_range_is_host(AbstractDelegationMap(dm@), KeyRange { lo: lo, hi: hi }, dst@),
{
}
reason: If delegation check is always true, the function never rejects an incorrect host assignment for a key range

name: is_end_always_true
reason: If is_end is always true then no KeyIterator holds a valid key, making get's precondition unsatisfiable

name: get_all_keys_zero
code:
proof fn phi_4_get_all_keys_zero(ki: KeyIterator<AbstractKey>)
    requires
        !ki.is_end_spec(),
    ensures
        *ki.get_spec() == AbstractKey::zero_spec(),
{
}
reason: If get always yields the zero key, the iterator cannot distinguish different keys and delegation lookups collapse

name: clone_collapses_keys
code:
proof fn phi_5_clone_collapses_keys(k1: SHTKey, k2: SHTKey)
    requires
        k1.ukey != k2.ukey,
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If distinct SHTKey values cannot exist, clone is trivially correct but the entire key-based delegation system is degenerate
